---
# EXECUTION-SPEC Frontmatter
# Generated by build-pipeline (lead-architect Stage 1 + inline expert refinement)

prd: PRD-092
prd_title: "POS Loyalty Instrument Printing — Linux Exemplar (Reference Implementation)"
service: LoyaltyService
submodule: InstrumentPrinting
mvp_phase: 3  # Rewards

# Authority chain (all FROZEN — record, do not re-litigate)
adr_refs: [ADR-062, ADR-063, ADR-045, ADR-058, ADR-024, ADR-030, ADR-020]
scaffold_ref: docs/01-scaffolds/SCAFFOLD-POS-LOYALTY-INSTRUMENT-PRINTING.md
feature_boundary: docs/20-architecture/specs/loyalty-printing/FEATURE_BOUNDARY.md
rfc_ref: docs/02-design/RFC-POS-LOYALTY-INSTRUMENT-PRINTING.md
sec_note: docs/20-architecture/specs/loyalty-printing/SEC_NOTE.md
# FIB intake: PRD-only mode (no structured_ref in PRD frontmatter); FIB-S boundary propagated via PRD/RFC/ADR.
fib_h: docs/00-vision/epson/FIB-H-POS-LOYALTY-INSTRUMENT-PRINTING-001.md
fib_s: docs/00-vision/epson/FIB-S-POS-LOYALTY-INSTRUMENT-PRINTING-001.json

phase_scope: linux_cups_exemplar   # ADR-062 D8 Phase 1 / Gate E1 ONLY — NO Windows certification
writes: [print_attempt]            # promo_coupon / promo_program READ-ONLY to this submodule
printer_target: configuration_only # ADR-062 D5 — no table this pilot

# Workstream Definitions (executor_type: skill = dispatched via Skill tool)
workstreams:
  WS1:
    name: print_attempt schema + RLS migration
    description: >
      Dedicated print_attempt relation — single audit-controlled lifecycle row per attempt (ADR-062 D2).
      POLYMORPHIC instrument correlation (DEC-003): instrument_kind ∈ {promo_coupon, ledger_entry} +
      instrument_ref uuid (CHECK on kind; NO hard FK — no-authoring invariant preserved). Immutable
      identity/correlation columns; mutable result_status + canonical failure {failure_domain, failure_code}
      (failure_domain CHECK permits render_validation | transport_submission [DEC-006]; 'device' forbidden
      this phase, §7a); server-derived idempotency_key (DEC-005) + reprint_of lineage. Identity/terminal
      immutability enforced by a BEFORE UPDATE TRIGGER (DA P1/§3), NOT by RLS. CONTROLLED-WRITE BOUNDARY
      (DEC-007): RLS DENIES direct INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE — the WS2 SECURITY DEFINER RPCs are the ONLY write
      path; RLS grants same-casino SELECT only. (A permissive direct-DML policy would let a client forge
      operator_id, mint an arbitrary idempotency key, or fabricate a 'submitted' row that never reached an
      adapter — P0-1.) Closed state machine: requested → exactly once → terminal {submitted|failed|unknown};
      terminal rows reject all mutation. Pattern C casino-scoped read RLS bundled in the SAME migration
      (ADR-015/020/024). UNIQUE (casino_id, idempotency_key). Index (casino_id, result_status, requested_at)
      for print-health signal + stuck-requested sweeps. Regenerate types after.
    executor: backend-service-builder
    executor_type: skill
    traces_to: [ADR-062-D2, ADR-062-D7, DEC-003, DEC-005, PRD-GATE-DOM-1, PRD-GATE-DUP-1, SEC-T5, SEC-T9]
    depends_on: []
    outputs:
      - supabase/migrations/YYYYMMDDHHMMSS_create_print_attempt.sql
      - types/database.types.ts
    gate: schema-validation
    estimated_complexity: high

  WS2:
    name: print_attempt controlled-write RPCs (SECURITY DEFINER)
    description: >
      Controlled-write + transition + reconciler RPCs (rpc_request_print_attempt,
      rpc_transition_print_attempt, rpc_mark_stale_print_attempts_unknown) — SECURITY DEFINER,
      SET search_path, PERFORM set_rls_context_from_staff() for authoritative context (ADR-024/ADR-030),
      role gate pit_boss|admin, REVOKE PUBLIC/anon + GRANT authenticated. RPCs accept NO operator_id/casino_id
      parameters — derived from context only (negative test asserts a forged actor is impossible, INCLUDING
      via direct table DML which is RLS-denied per DEC-007). request INSERTs print_attempt(requested) with the
      SERVER-DERIVED idempotency key (DEC-005); first_print replay returns the prior row, reprint-nonce forks
      a new instance. REFERENTIAL GUARD (P0-2/DEC-003): because there is no hard FK, the request RPC resolves
      instrument_ref to a real SAME-CASINO promo_coupon|loyalty_ledger row of the declared kind BEFORE INSERT
      (P0003 on miss) — the write boundary owns integrity, not the route. transition mutates ONLY
      result_status/failure; the WS1 trigger rejects terminal-row/identity mutation. rpc_mark_stale_print_
      attempts_unknown (P0-4) is a bounded reconciler: frozen 15-min threshold, caller-casino `requested`
      rows → `unknown` only, FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED, request-time/admin invocation (NO scheduler). Plus RLS
      cross-casino isolation tests + forged-actor + direct-DML-denied negative tests.
    executor: rls-expert
    executor_type: skill
    traces_to: [ADR-062-D2, ADR-063-D5, ADR-024, ADR-030, DEC-003, DEC-005, DEC-006, DEC-007, PRD-NFR-3, SEC-T5, SEC-T6, PRD-GATE-DUP-1]
    depends_on: [WS1]
    outputs:
      - supabase/migrations/YYYYMMDDHHMMSS_create_print_attempt_write_rpcs.sql
      - __tests__/services/loyalty/printing/print-attempt-rls.int.test.ts
    gate: type-check
    estimated_complexity: high

  WS3:
    name: InstrumentPrinting service submodule + LoyaltyInstrumentPrinter contract
    description: >
      services/loyalty/printing/ submodule (functional factory, Pattern A, explicit interface). OS-neutral
      LoyaltyInstrumentPrinter contract { getStatus, print, testPrint } (ADR-062 D3). Four-state result
      vocabulary (requested|submitted|failed|unknown — submitted ≠ printed) + canonical failure types
      (render_validation domain ONLY this phase; device/PrinterFault MUST be absent — §7a deferral).
      print_attempt dtos/mappers/keys/schemas derived from Database types. Adapter-registry interface
      (selected at deployment time, not runtime hot-swap). NO CUPS types in this layer (GATE-PLATFORM-1).
    executor: backend-service-builder
    executor_type: skill
    traces_to: [ADR-062-D3, PRD-NFR-1, PRD-GATE-PLATFORM-1, PRD-TERMINOLOGY-7a]
    depends_on: [WS1]
    outputs:
      - services/loyalty/printing/index.ts
      - services/loyalty/printing/contract.ts
      - services/loyalty/printing/dtos.ts
      - services/loyalty/printing/schemas.ts
      - services/loyalty/printing/mappers.ts
      - services/loyalty/printing/README.md
    # NOTE (DA §8): keys.ts (React Query key factory) DEFERRED — single mutation surface, no list/detail
    # query in scope; YAGNI until the deferred print-health dashboard arrives.
    gate: type-check
    estimated_complexity: medium

  WS4:
    name: Receipt templating layer (ReceiptDocument + renderers)
    description: >
      Versioned template builder consuming the FROZEN FulfillmentPayload (services/loyalty/dtos.ts:623 —
      reused, NOT forked) → canonical transport-neutral ReceiptDocument (block list: text/divider/image/
      barcode/feed/cut) (ADR-062 D6). VALUE AUTHORITY (DA §5): the template renders value from the
      FulfillmentPayload (face_value_cents — integer cents) and NEVER re-derives from promo_coupon
      (face_value_amount numeric dollars — different unit); receipt_document_hash provenance is over the
      payload-derived canonical document. Renderers: cups (production payload), fake (deterministic snapshot),
      html-preview (preview-only, non-canonical — never the production payload, GATE-UX-1). Builders are
      PURE (no instrument mutation, no network, no printer/OS/window.print calls). receipt_document_hash =
      digest of the canonical serialized ReceiptDocument (NOT the transport payload). Templates are
      version-controlled code (vN immutable; changes create vN+1). NO runtime/admin template engine.
    executor: backend-service-builder
    executor_type: skill
    traces_to: [ADR-062-D6, PRD-FR-6, PRD-FR-7, PRD-GATE-UX-1]
    depends_on: [WS3]
    outputs:
      - services/loyalty/printing/templates/receipt-document.ts
      - services/loyalty/printing/templates/build-receipt.ts
      - services/loyalty/printing/renderers/cups-renderer.ts
      - services/loyalty/printing/renderers/fake-renderer.ts
      - services/loyalty/printing/renderers/html-preview-renderer.ts
      - services/loyalty/printing/templates/hash.ts
    gate: type-check
    estimated_complexity: high

  WS5:
    name: Transport adapters (cups + fake) + loopback exemplar agent
    description: >
      Two adapters behind LoyaltyInstrumentPrinter — cups (one-way CUPS spooler submission; spooler-accept
      → submitted; any spooler "completed" maps to submitted, NEVER a stronger state) and fake
      (deterministic). OS-neutrality is a hard boundary: zero CUPS-specific types/strings/IDs above the
      adapter (GATE-PLATFORM-1). CALL-PATH (DEC-002): the cups adapter runs SERVER-SIDE and POSTs the job to
      the loopback agent (127.0.0.1) which fronts CUPS and returns the spooler outcome; the server (WS6) owns
      the audit write + terminal transition. The exemplar agent binds loopback ONLY (NFR-4) and honors
      idempotency (ADR-063 D5) + truthful failure mapping (ADR-063 D6) — the ONLY ADR-063 decisions in scope.
      Device faults out of scope (failure_domain=device stays null).
    executor: backend-service-builder
    executor_type: skill
    traces_to: [ADR-062-D3, ADR-063-D5, ADR-063-D6, PRD-NFR-2, PRD-NFR-4, PRD-GATE-PLATFORM-1, PRD-GATE-PLATFORM-2]
    depends_on: [WS3, WS4]
    outputs:
      - services/loyalty/printing/adapters/cups-adapter.ts
      - services/loyalty/printing/adapters/fake-adapter.ts
      - services/loyalty/printing/agent/loopback-agent.ts
      - services/loyalty/printing/adapters/index.ts
    gate: type-check
    estimated_complexity: high

  WS6:
    name: Controlled print action + admin test-print
    description: >
      Controlled print action (route handler) — validate target+payload availability → rpc_request_print_
      attempt(requested) with the SERVER-DERIVED idempotency key (DEC-005) → cups adapter (server-side, POSTs
      to loopback agent per DEC-002) → rpc_transition_print_attempt(terminal). FAILS CLOSED before any success
      claim (FIB feature-loop step 3). Render/validation failures caught BEFORE the adapter → failed /
      render_validation (FR-6). RUNTIME renderer guard (DA §3/FR-7): the production action REJECTS any
      non-canonical renderer (html-preview never reaches the device) — runtime check, not just lint.
      Idempotent first_print replay returns the prior outcome (no second copy); explicit reprint carries a
      reprint nonce → new attempt instance sharing instrument identity (GATE-DUP-1). COLLISION SEMANTICS
      (DA P1-B / DEC-005): the UNIQUE(casino_id, idempotency_key) INSERT is the single-flight primitive —
      terminal prior row → return prior (no adapter call); in-flight (requested) prior row → REJECT
      (no adapter call); the adapter is invoked EXACTLY ONCE, by the INSERT-winner only, so a double-click
      cannot mint a second physical copy. AUTHORITATIVE instrument_ref integrity now lives in
      rpc_request_print_attempt (P0-2/DEC-007): the RPC resolves instrument_ref to a same-casino instrument of
      the declared kind and rejects a non-resolving/cross-casino ref (P0003) BEFORE any write. The route MAY
      do an early read-only lookup purely for fast user-facing error mapping, but it is NOT the integrity
      boundary and MUST NOT be relied on as one. Accepts polymorphic instrument ref
      (DEC-003: promo_coupon | ledger_entry). Non-redeemable admin test-print path exercises the adapter
      DIRECTLY without issuing an instrument and WITHOUT calling rpc_request_print_attempt (no audit row;
      feature-loop step 8). NO public printer endpoint;
      device address never reaches the browser (opaque printer_target_id only). NEVER writes promo_coupon /
      loyalty ledger (GATE-DOM-1). ServiceHttpResult contract (lib/http/service-response.ts).
    executor: api-builder
    executor_type: skill
    traces_to: [ADR-062-D2, ADR-062-D3, ADR-063-D5, ADR-063-D6, DEC-002, DEC-003, DEC-005, PRD-FR-2, PRD-FR-4, PRD-FR-5, PRD-FR-6, PRD-FR-7, PRD-GATE-DOM-1, PRD-GATE-DUP-1, SEC-T1, SEC-T4, SEC-T8]
    depends_on: [WS2, WS3, WS5]
    outputs:
      - app/api/v1/loyalty/printing/route.ts
      - app/api/v1/loyalty/printing/test-print/route.ts
      - services/loyalty/printing/http.ts
    gate: lint
    estimated_complexity: high

  WS7:
    name: Operator UX — retire window.print() on named surface
    description: >
      On components/player-360/header/issue-reward-button.tsx (the named redemption surface that owns
      usePrintReward) replace the window.print() path with the controlled action for BOTH families (DEC-003:
      entitlement + comp slips both route through the controlled path) — GATE-UX-1: 0 window.print calls on
      THIS surface. MANUAL-FIRST (DEC-004): DROP the auto_attempt on-issuance auto-print; the operator must
      explicitly click Print (no print fires without an operator gesture). Bounded outcome badge (submitted |
      failed | unknown — "submitted" copy must NOT imply physical completion). TERMINAL RETRY SEMANTICS
      (DEC-008): failed and unknown are BOTH terminal+immutable, so there is NO "Retry" affordance — replaying
      the first_print key only returns the prior terminal row (the WS1 trigger forbids mutating it). Any second
      physical submission is the explicit, nonce-bearing REPRINT only (DEC-005); for `unknown` the Reprint
      requires a duplicate-risk acknowledgement (the job MAY have printed). An optional non-printing Refresh
      Status is permitted. NO silent auto-retry; NO automatic browser fallback (GATE-DUP-1, ADR-062 D4 /
      ADR-063 D7). lib/print/ window.print() + auto_attempt remain UNTOUCHED for non-loyalty docs (MTL/shift
      reports). Operator-copy review per §7a.
    executor: frontend-design-pt-2
    executor_type: skill
    traces_to: [ADR-062-D4, DEC-003, DEC-004, DEC-005, PRD-FR-1, PRD-FR-3, PRD-GATE-UX-1, PRD-GATE-DUP-1, PRD-TERMINOLOGY-7a, ADR-063-D7]
    depends_on: [WS6]
    outputs:
      - components/player-360/header/issue-reward-button.tsx
      - components/loyalty/print-outcome-badge.tsx
      - hooks/loyalty/use-controlled-print.ts
    gate: lint
    estimated_complexity: medium

  WS8:
    name: Test + boundary-lint suite
    description: >
      One contract-parity suite both cups AND fake pass (GATE-PLATFORM-2) returning the canonical
      result/failure vocabulary, runnable WITHOUT a physical printer (GATE-PLATFORM-1). ReceiptDocument
      semantic snapshot (instrument id, value, expiry, terms, token, template id/version, feed/cut — ADR-062
      D6). GATE-DOM-1 invariant test: zero promo_coupon/ledger writes across ALL outcomes (requested|
      submitted|failed|unknown). GATE-DUP-1 (DA-expanded): (a) idempotent first_print replay emits one copy;
      (b) reprint nonce = distinct lineage; (c) NO auto-print fires on issuance (DEC-004); (d) single-flight
      under two CONCURRENT first_print requests — asserts the ADAPTER SPY is invoked EXACTLY ONCE (not merely
      that one HTTP response is a rejection), terminal→return-prior / in-flight→reject (DA P1-B / DEC-005).
      Idempotency-key derivation test: retry collapses, reprint forks (DEC-005). CONTROLLED-WRITE BOUNDARY
      test (P0-1/DEC-007): a direct authenticated `.from('print_attempt').insert()` AND `.update()` are RLS-
      DENIED — the RPCs are the only write path. instrument_ref resolution test (P0-2/DEC-003): a non-resolving
      / cross-casino instrument_ref is rejected by rpc_request_print_attempt (P0003) BEFORE any print_attempt
      write. Comp-family (ledger_entry) controlled-path parity test (DA §7): the points_comp family routes
      through the controlled path and persists a print_attempt with instrument_kind=ledger_entry (the family
      DEC-003 exists to support is exercised, not only entitlement). Render-validation-before-adapter test:
      invalid ReceiptDocument → failed/render_validation, adapter never invoked (FR-6). Transport-failure test
      (DEC-006): an unreachable agent / spooler-reject transitions to failed/transport_submission (NOT a null
      domain). Orphaned-requested reconciliation test (P0-4): rpc_mark_stale_print_attempts_unknown sweeps a
      stale `requested` row to `unknown`. Boundary lint (TEMPLATE-2 / NFR-5): no Epson/ESC-POS/CUPS/spooler/
      window.print outside renderer/adapter layer. §7a deferral assertion: failure_domain=device never
      populated; PrinterFault vocabulary absent from DTOs/schema. All *.int.test.ts MUST be real-DB (Gate A
      non-waivable).
    executor: qa-specialist
    executor_type: skill
    traces_to: [PRD-GATE-PLATFORM-1, PRD-GATE-PLATFORM-2, PRD-GATE-DOM-1, PRD-GATE-DUP-1, PRD-FR-6, PRD-NFR-5, ADR-062-D6, DEC-004, DEC-005, DEC-006, DEC-007, DEC-008, PRD-TERMINOLOGY-7a]
    depends_on: [WS3, WS4, WS5, WS6, WS7]
    outputs:
      - __tests__/services/loyalty/printing/printer-contract-parity.test.ts
      - __tests__/services/loyalty/printing/receipt-document-snapshot.test.ts
      - __tests__/services/loyalty/printing/no-instrument-authoring.int.test.ts
      - __tests__/services/loyalty/printing/duplicate-safety.int.test.ts
      - __tests__/services/loyalty/printing/idempotency-key-derivation.int.test.ts
      - __tests__/services/loyalty/printing/controlled-write-boundary.int.test.ts
      - __tests__/services/loyalty/printing/instrument-ref-resolution.int.test.ts
      - __tests__/services/loyalty/printing/comp-family-controlled-path.int.test.ts
      - __tests__/services/loyalty/printing/render-validation-fail-closed.int.test.ts
      - __tests__/services/loyalty/printing/transport-failure-domain.int.test.ts
      - __tests__/services/loyalty/printing/stale-reconciliation.int.test.ts
      - eslint.config.mjs
    gate: test-pass
    estimated_complexity: high

  WS9:
    name: E2E write-path (happy path)
    description: >
      Playwright happy-path: operator issues an entitlement instrument on the redemption surface → clicks the
      controlled Print action → outcome resolves submitted → a print_attempt row is persisted with full
      correlation. Satisfies the write-path E2E mandate and PRD §8 Testing.
    executor: e2e-testing
    executor_type: skill
    traces_to: [PRD-GATE-UX-1, PRD-DOD-E2E]
    depends_on: [WS6, WS7]
    outputs:
      - e2e/loyalty-printing/controlled-print-happy-path.spec.ts
    gate: e2e-write-path
    estimated_complexity: medium

# Execution Phases (topologically sorted; parallel where dependency-free)
execution_phases:
  - name: Phase 1 - Audit Foundation
    parallel: [WS1]
    gates: [schema-validation]

  - name: Phase 2 - Write Path + Contract
    parallel: [WS2, WS3]
    gates: [type-check]

  - name: Phase 3 - Templating
    parallel: [WS4]
    gates: [type-check]

  - name: Phase 4 - Transport
    parallel: [WS5]
    gates: [type-check]

  - name: Phase 5 - Controlled Action
    parallel: [WS6]
    gates: [lint]

  - name: Phase 6 - Operator Surface
    parallel: [WS7]
    gates: [lint]

  - name: Phase 7 - Test + Boundary-Lint Suite
    # WS8 now depends_on WS7 (it asserts the auto-print removal WS7 owns), so it
    # runs AFTER the surface rather than in parallel with it.
    parallel: [WS8]
    gates: [test-pass]

  - name: Phase 8 - E2E
    parallel: [WS9]
    gates: [e2e-write-path]

# Validation Gates
gates:
  schema-validation:
    command: npm run db:types-local
    success_criteria: "Exit code 0, print_attempt types generated, no type errors"
  type-check:
    command: npm run type-check
    success_criteria: "Exit code 0"
  lint:
    command: npm run lint
    success_criteria: "Exit code 0, max-warnings=0"
  test-pass:
    # DA P1-A: bare `npm test` runs jest.config.js (jsdom) ONLY and ignores *.int.test.ts
    # (package.json test:ci explicitly ignores \.int\.test). The integration safety invariants
    # (GATE-DOM-1, GATE-DUP-1, idempotency-derivation, render-validation-fail-closed) live in
    # *.int.test.ts and execute ONLY under jest.integration.config.js with RUN_INTEGRATION_TESTS=true.
    # gate_b_classification is "none" for this build, so Gate B's forcing never fires — this gate
    # is the ONLY place the int tests are made to actually run. Two invocations, fail-closed on stack.
    command_unit: npm test -- __tests__/services/loyalty/printing/
    command_integration: >
      npx supabase status -o env > /tmp/exec092-stack.env &&
      RUN_INTEGRATION_TESTS=true npx jest --config jest.integration.config.js
      --testPathPatterns='services/loyalty/printing/' --runInBand --ci
      --listTests > /tmp/exec092-int-discovered.log 2>&1 &&
      RUN_INTEGRATION_TESTS=true npx jest --config jest.integration.config.js
      --testPathPatterns='services/loyalty/printing/' --runInBand --ci
      > /tmp/exec092-integration.log 2>&1
    stack_probe: "npx supabase status — fail-closed: stack DOWN → BLOCK (never skip-green); operator runs `npx supabase start`"
    # P1-2: use `npx jest` (repo binary, not PATH-resolved), and assert the EXPECTED int files were
    # discovered (not merely ≥1). Required executed set (the *.int.test.ts in WS2/WS8 outputs):
    #   print-attempt-rls, controlled-write-boundary, no-instrument-authoring, duplicate-safety,
    #   idempotency-key-derivation, instrument-ref-resolution, comp-family-controlled-path,
    #   render-validation-fail-closed, transport-failure-domain, stale-reconciliation.
    discovery_assertion: "Every required *.int.test.ts above appears in /tmp/exec092-int-discovered.log; a missing file fails the gate (catches a silently-undiscovered or renamed test)."
    success_criteria: "Both runs exit 0; the discovery list contains every required *.int.test.ts; the integration run EXECUTED them (not skipped / not 0-total); GATE-DOM-1 + GATE-DUP-1 + boundary + contract-parity green under real DB"
  e2e-write-path:
    command: npx playwright test e2e/loyalty-printing/ --reporter=list
    success_criteria: "At least 1 spec exists, all tests pass"

# Manual Gates (NOT automatable workstreams)
manual_gates:
  - id: GATE-HW-2
    name: Real TM-T88V Linux/CUPS device exemplar
    type: manual_operational_acceptance
    traces_to: ADR-062-D8 (Gate E1)
    evidence: "first-print, printer-offline, retry, deliberate reprint, cut + layout — each distinguishable on the Linux/CUPS rig"
    owner: operator/floor-tech on the configured TM-T88V (UB-U05 USB) rig

# Open-Question Decisions
decisions:
  - decision_id: DEC-001
    decision_statement: "SUPERSEDED by DEC-003. (Original: instrument_id → promo_coupon FK, comp family out of scope.)"
    rationale: "DA P0-1: the named surface (issue-reward-button.tsx) prints BOTH entitlement coupons AND comp slips (lib/print/print-reward.ts dual-family dispatch). Excluding comps while claiming 0 window.print() on that surface (GATE-UX-1) is contradictory. Resolved by DEC-003."
    impact_on_scope: superseded
    verification: cited
  - decision_id: DEC-003
    supersedes: DEC-001
    decision_statement: "print_attempt correlates to the instrument via a POLYMORPHIC reference: instrument_kind ∈ {promo_coupon, ledger_entry} + instrument_ref uuid (NO hard FK). Both families (entitlement coupons → promo_coupon, points_comp slips → loyalty ledger entry) route through the controlled path, satisfying GATE-UX-1 (0 window.print on the named surface). promo_coupon / loyalty_ledger remain READ-ONLY (no FK write-back; the no-authoring invariant is unchanged)."
    rationale: "DA P0-1 resolution (user-approved). The named surface prints both families; a hard promo_coupon FK cannot represent a comp slip. A discriminated reference keeps the audit correlation while still authoring no instrument. CHECK constraint validates instrument_kind; a deferred reconciliation can confirm referential validity without a cross-family FK."
    impact_on_scope: none
    verification: cited
  - decision_id: DEC-002
    resolves_open_question: "OQ-1"
    decision_statement: "Exemplar co-locates dev-server + loopback agent + CUPS on one Linux host. CALL-PATH (DA P1-3): the SERVER route handler writes print_attempt(requested) and performs the terminal transition (authoritative audit); the cups adapter runs SERVER-SIDE and POSTs the job to the loopback agent (127.0.0.1) which fronts CUPS and returns the spooler outcome to the server. The agent honors ADR-063 D5 (idempotency) + D6 (truthful failure) only; D1–D4/D7 hardening deferred to PRD #2 (Windows)."
    rationale: "Keeps the audit server-authoritative (ADR-024 context) AND makes the loopback agent (NFR-4) load-bearing rather than vestigial. Lightest realization proving the managed-local-agent transport + in-scope ADR-063 behaviors."
    impact_on_scope: none
    verification: assumption
  - decision_id: DEC-004
    decision_statement: "Manual-first only (DA P1-1). The controlled path DROPS auto_attempt: the operator must explicitly click Print. No print fires on issuance without an operator gesture."
    rationale: "Strongest duplicate-safety on a redeemable instrument; aligns with PRD §6 ('operator … clicks Print') and ADR-045 D1A manual-first. Removes the auto-print-then-manual double-copy vector. lib/print/ auto_attempt remains for non-loyalty surfaces (untouched)."
    impact_on_scope: none
    verification: cited
  - decision_id: DEC-005
    decision_statement: "Idempotency key is SERVER-DERIVED (DA P1-2): key = hash(instrument_kind, instrument_ref, intent) where intent='first_print' collapses all retries of the first print onto one row; an explicit reprint carries an operator-supplied reprint nonce → distinct key → new attempt instance sharing instrument identity (GATE-DUP-1). The client never mints the key. COLLISION SEMANTICS (DA P1-B): on a UNIQUE(casino_id, idempotency_key) collision the INSERT is the single-flight primitive — if the existing row is TERMINAL {submitted|failed|unknown} the prior row is returned and the adapter is NOT invoked (idempotent replay); if the existing row is still in-flight (requested) the request is REJECTED with a single-flight error and the adapter is NOT invoked. The adapter is invoked EXACTLY ONCE, by the INSERT-winner only."
    rationale: "A client-minted key lets a buggy retry with a fresh key mint a second copy. Server derivation makes retry-collapse and reprint-fork deterministic and testable. 'Return prior outcome' (terminal) and 'reject concurrent in-flight' (requested) are the SAME UNIQUE collision resolved by row state — the load-bearing invariant is adapter-fires-once-by-the-winner, so a double-click cannot mint a second physical copy."
    impact_on_scope: none
    verification: cited
  - decision_id: DEC-006
    decision_statement: "failure_domain admits a non-device 'transport_submission' domain (codes: agent_unreachable | spooler_rejected | malformed_agent_response) IN ADDITION to 'render_validation'. Pre-submission transport faults (agent unreachable, spooler reject) are terminal `failed` + transport_submission; an ambiguous post-submission loss is `unknown` (the job MAY have printed), not `failed`. 'device' remains FORBIDDEN this phase (§7a)."
    rationale: "Audit P1-1: WS6 maps unreachable-agent / spooler-reject to `failed`, but the original render_validation-only CHECK forced those onto a NULL domain — a `failed` row with no domain breaks the PRD's claimed canonical failure contract. transport_submission is a transport fault, NOT device-fault fidelity, so it does not reopen the §7a device deferral. This consciously amends the 'only render_validation populated this phase' guardrail (user-approved)."
    amends: "phase_scope_guardrails ('Only failure_domain=render_validation populated this phase')"
    impact_on_scope: minor
    verification: cited
  - decision_id: DEC-007
    decision_statement: "print_attempt is an RPC-ONLY controlled-write table. RLS DENIES direct INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE to all authenticated roles (SELECT same-casino remains); the WS2 SECURITY DEFINER RPCs (which bypass RLS) are the sole write path. The instrument_ref referential guard (DEC-003, no hard FK) lives INSIDE rpc_request_print_attempt — resolution to a same-casino instrument of the declared kind, rejecting non-resolving/cross-casino refs (P0003) before INSERT — NOT in the route. The route may keep an early read-only lookup for UX error mapping only."
    rationale: "Audit P0-1/P0-2: WS1 originally granted permissive direct INSERT/UPDATE to pit_boss/admin while WS2 claimed the RPCs were the only sanctioned route — a hard contradiction. The direct INSERT path (a) was not trigger-guarded (trigger is BEFORE UPDATE only), (b) did not bind operator_id to context, and (c) skipped idempotency + instrument resolution — enabling a forged-operator, fabricated-'submitted' audit row. With no hard FK, the DB write boundary must own referential integrity, so a direct RPC call cannot mint a dangling/cross-casino correlation either."
    impact_on_scope: none
    verification: cited
  - decision_id: DEC-008
    decision_statement: "failed AND unknown are both terminal+immutable. There is NO 'Retry' that re-drives the first_print key (it would only return the prior terminal row, or demand mutating a frozen row — forbidden by the WS1 trigger). Any second physical submission is the explicit, nonce-bearing REPRINT only; for `unknown`, Reprint requires a duplicate-risk acknowledgement. An optional non-printing Refresh Status is allowed."
    rationale: "Audit P0-3: WS7 originally exposed 'Retry on failed/unknown' that 're-drives the same key', but DEC-005 (return-prior on terminal replay) + the trigger (terminal rows immutable) make that a dead end that strands the operator. Resubmission-only-via-Reprint keeps the closed-state machine intact without making `failed` mutable."
    impact_on_scope: none
    verification: cited

# Open Questions (carried)
open_questions:
  - id: OQ-1
    question: "Server↔agent topology + call-path for the exemplar."
    disposition: resolved (DEC-002 — server-authoritative audit; server-side adapter POSTs to loopback agent)

# External dependencies
external_dependencies:
  - prd: PRD-052
    service: LoyaltyService
    required_for: "Frozen FulfillmentPayload print contract (services/loyalty/dtos.ts:623) — reused, not forked"
  - adr: ADR-045
    service: LoyaltyService
    required_for: "lib/print/ window.print() utility retained for non-loyalty docs (coexistence)"

# Risks and Mitigations
risks:
  - risk: "CUPS-semantic leakage into the contract would break the future Windows port (GATE-PLATFORM-1)."
    mitigation: "Boundary lint (WS8/TEMPLATE-2) + contract-parity suite run against fake with no CUPS imports above the adapter."
  - risk: "A retry/reprint handler accidentally re-invokes issuance → duplicate redeemable instrument (SEC T1/T7)."
    mitigation: "GATE-DOM-1 invariant test (zero promo_coupon writes on all outcomes) + idempotency key + explicit-reprint-only UX (no silent auto-retry, no auto window.print fallback)."
  - risk: "Provisional 80mm layout mistaken for production-authoritative content (FIB DEP-3 deferred)."
    mitigation: "Layout visibly marked non-authoritative; authoritative legal text/symbology deferred to PRD #2 per PRD §7."
  - risk: "PrinterFault / device-fault vocabulary creeping into DTOs/UI/schema (§7a hard deferral)."
    mitigation: "Deferral assertion test (failure_domain=device never populated; PrinterFault absent) + operator-copy review."
  - risk: "Auto-print (auto_attempt) + manual click mint two physical copies on a redeemable instrument (DA P1-1)."
    mitigation: "DEC-004 drops auto_attempt on the controlled path (manual-first); GATE-DUP-1 test (c) asserts no print fires on issuance."
  - risk: "Client-minted idempotency key lets a buggy retry mint a second copy (DA P1-2)."
    mitigation: "DEC-005 server-derived key (instrument_kind+instrument_ref+intent); derivation test proves retry-collapse vs reprint-fork."
---

# EXECUTION-SPEC: PRD-092 — POS Loyalty Instrument Printing (Linux Exemplar)

## Overview

This EXEC delivers the **Linux/CUPS exemplar (reference implementation)** of the controlled loyalty-instrument
print path per **ADR-062 D8 Phase 1 / Gate E1**. It replaces the browser `window.print()` path on the named
loyalty **redemption** surface with a controlled action that targets a configured printer, persists a durable
`print_attempt` audit row, and returns a truthful four-state outcome (`requested | submitted | failed |
unknown`). **Success means the architecture is correct — not that Windows is certified.** This is **not
primarily wiring**: it stands up a new audit relation, a closed lifecycle state machine, an OS-neutral printer
contract, a versioned templating layer, two transport adapters, and a duplicate-safe operator workflow.

Windows production certification (the `windows_spooler` adapter, install/update ownership, full ADR-063 D1–D4/D7
hardening, localhost security review) is **explicitly out of scope** — it is a separate follow-on PRD (#2 /
Gate E2 / `WINDOWS_CERTIFICATION_REQUIRED`).

## Scope

**In scope (Linux exemplar):**
- `print_attempt` relation (single audit-controlled lifecycle row per attempt) + Pattern C casino-scoped RLS +
  SECURITY DEFINER controlled-write/transition RPCs.
- OS-neutral `LoyaltyInstrumentPrinter` contract + four-state result + render_validation failure vocabulary.
- Versioned templating: `FulfillmentPayload` → canonical `ReceiptDocument` → renderers (`cups`/`fake`/`html-preview`).
- Two adapters (`cups` production realization + `fake` deterministic) behind the contract; loopback exemplar
  agent honoring ADR-063 D5/D6 only.
- Controlled print action + non-redeemable admin test-print.
- Operator UX retiring `window.print()` on the named redemption surface only; explicit retry/reprint.
- Contract-parity + invariant + duplicate-safety tests; boundary lint; happy-path E2E.

**Out of scope (hard exclusions):**
- Windows / `windows_spooler` adapter, install/update procedures, Windows localhost security review (PRD #2).
- Device-fault fidelity (`failure_domain=device` stays null; one-way CUPS cannot observe it) — `submitted`-only ceiling.
- Bidirectional ESC/POS, ePOS-direct, fleet/cloud queue, multi-vendor abstraction, unattended/batch printing.
- `PrinterFault` / device-fault vocabulary in DTOs/UI/schema (§7a hard deferral). `ReceiptDocument` used locally, NOT SRL-admitted.
- Generic/runtime/admin template engine; authoritative legal layout + barcode symbology (PRD #2 / FIB DEP-3, GAP-C5).
- Any change to `lib/print/` `window.print()` for non-loyalty docs (shift reports, MTL) — retained untouched.
- Any change to instrument eligibility/value/redemption authority; no new instrument types.

## Architecture Context

- **Ownership (ADR-062 D1 / Feature Boundary):** `LoyaltyService.InstrumentPrinting` submodule — no standalone
  service, no new SRM row, no bounded-context admission. Writes ONLY `print_attempt`; `promo_coupon` /
  `promo_program` read-only.
- **Classification (ADR-058):** CLS-007 external_integration (primary) + CLS-003 authoring (secondary).
  `selected_transport: external_integration` → managed local agent → CUPS spooler (Linux exemplar). No
  transactional outbox (`finance_outbox` MUST NOT carry print events).
- **Terminology governance (PRD §7a):** `srl_required: false` — print-lifecycle terms are local
  integration-contract vocabulary governed by the local Terminology/Operator-Copy Gate, NOT SRL admission.
  The six local terms (`requested|submitted|failed|unknown`, `retry`, `reprint`) must be defined in the
  glossary and reflected consistently across DTOs, API responses, tests, and UI copy. `submitted` ≠ printed.
- **Security (SEC Note / ADR-024 / ADR-030):** authoritative `operator_id`/`casino_id` from session-var
  context; `print_attempt` casino-scoped (Pattern C); no device address or secret in the browser bundle;
  exemplar agent binds loopback only (GATE-SEC-1 minimal).

## Integration Points (verified in repo)

| Concern | Location | Action |
|---|---|---|
| Named redemption surface (owns `usePrintReward`) | `components/player-360/header/issue-reward-button.tsx:~84` | Replace `window.print()` path with controlled action (WS7) |
| Existing browser-print stack (RETAINED) | `lib/print/` (iframe-print, print-reward, hooks, templates) | Untouched — serves non-loyalty docs |
| Source content payload (REUSE, do NOT fork) | `services/loyalty/dtos.ts:623` `FulfillmentPayload` | Input to templating builder (WS4) |
| Instrument source-of-truth (READ-ONLY) | `promo_coupon` (`20260106235611_loyalty_promo_instruments.sql`, PK `id`) + loyalty ledger | Polymorphic correlation target for `instrument_ref` (DEC-003); both READ-ONLY |
| Shipped auto-print + dual-family dispatch | `issue-reward-button.tsx:92` (`auto_attempt`), `lib/print/print-reward.ts` (comp+entitlement) | Auto-print DROPPED on controlled path (DEC-004); both families routed (DEC-003) |
| Service factory pattern | `services/loyalty/index.ts` (functional factory, Pattern A) | Mirror for `services/loyalty/printing/` (WS3) |
| Controlled-write RPC pattern | `20260326020531_prd059_open_custody_rpcs.sql` | `set_rls_context_from_staff()` + role gate + P-codes (WS2) |

## Workstream Details

### WS1: print_attempt schema + RLS migration
**Purpose:** Foundation audit relation with closed lifecycle + casino-scoped RLS.
**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] `print_attempt` table: immutable identity/correlation (`print_attempt_id` PK, **`instrument_kind`** ∈ {`promo_coupon`,`ledger_entry`} + **`instrument_ref`** uuid (CHECK on kind; NO hard FK — DEC-003), `casino_id`, `operator_id`, `printer_target_id`, `station_id` nullable, `requested_at`, `template_id`, `template_version`, `receipt_document_hash`); mutable `result_status` (`requested|submitted|failed|unknown`), `failure_domain` (`render_validation` \| `transport_submission` — DEC-006; CHECK forbids `device` this phase), `failure_code`; server-derived `idempotency_key`, `reprint_of` lineage.
- [ ] **BEFORE UPDATE trigger** enforces: closed state machine (`requested` → exactly once → terminal); terminal rows reject all mutation; identity/correlation columns immutable after insert. (Column immutability is the TRIGGER's job — NOT RLS.)
- [ ] **Controlled-write boundary (DEC-007):** RLS grants same-casino **SELECT only**; direct INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE are DENIED (`WITH CHECK (false)` / `USING (false)`). The WS2 SECURITY DEFINER RPCs (which bypass RLS) are the sole write path — no permissive direct-DML policy exists.
- [ ] Unique constraint on `(casino_id, idempotency_key)`; index `(casino_id, result_status, requested_at)` for print-health + stuck-requested sweeps.
- [ ] `npm run db:types-local` succeeds; `print_attempt` types generated.

### WS2: Controlled-write RPCs (SECURITY DEFINER)
**Purpose:** Authoritative-context write path; the only sanctioned mutation route.
**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] `rpc_request_print_attempt` inserts `requested` with context-bound `operator_id`/`casino_id` (`set_rls_context_from_staff()`), role gate `pit_boss|admin`, `SET search_path`, REVOKE PUBLIC/anon + GRANT authenticated, idempotency dedup (returns prior row on key replay — ADR-063 D5).
- [ ] **Referential guard inside the RPC (P0-2/DEC-007):** before INSERT, `rpc_request_print_attempt` resolves `instrument_ref` to a real SAME-CASINO row on `promo_coupon` (kind=`promo_coupon`) or `loyalty_ledger` (kind=`ledger_entry`); a non-resolving / cross-casino ref raises `P0003` before any write. (No hard FK exists — DEC-003 — so the write boundary owns integrity, not the route.)
- [ ] `rpc_transition_print_attempt` mutates ONLY `result_status`/`failure`; raises on terminal-row mutation or identity rewrite; explicit reprint creates a NEW instance (never mutates a prior terminal row).
- [ ] **`rpc_mark_stale_print_attempts_unknown` (P0-4):** bounded reconciler — frozen 15-min threshold, caller-casino `requested` rows → `unknown` only, `FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED`, role gate `pit_boss|admin`, REVOKE PUBLIC/anon + GRANT authenticated; request-time/admin invocation (NO scheduler). Returns the reconciled count.
- [ ] RLS isolation int-test: cross-casino read/write denied; forged `operator_id` rejected (bound to context, not payload); **direct authenticated `.from('print_attempt').insert()`/`.update()` are RLS-denied (DEC-007).**

### WS3: InstrumentPrinting service submodule + contract
**Purpose:** OS-neutral application contract + Pattern-A service modules.
**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] `LoyaltyInstrumentPrinter { getStatus, print, testPrint }` with four-state result + render_validation failure types. No `acknowledged`/`printed`/`completed` state.
- [ ] `print_attempt` DTOs derive from `Database` types (Pick/Omit); camelCase; explicit interface; no `ReturnType`.
- [ ] Zero CUPS/Epson/ESC-POS types in this layer (GATE-PLATFORM-1).
- [ ] PrinterFault/device vocabulary ABSENT (§7a).

### WS4: Receipt templating layer
**Purpose:** Versioned canonical document between frozen payload and adapter.
**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Builder consumes `FulfillmentPayload` (reused), is PURE, emits canonical `ReceiptDocument` (block list).
- [ ] `cups` / `fake` / `html-preview` renderers; html-preview labelled preview-only and unreachable on the production path (FR-7).
- [ ] `receipt_document_hash` = digest of canonical serialized `ReceiptDocument` (NOT transport payload).
- [ ] `template_version` immutable (vN+1 on change); stored as code, not DB-authored.

### WS5: Transport adapters + loopback exemplar agent
**Purpose:** One production realization (`cups`) + deterministic `fake`, behind the contract.
**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Both adapters satisfy the contract; spooler-accept → `submitted` (never stronger); any "completed" maps to `submitted`.
- [ ] No CUPS-specific types/strings above the adapter boundary (GATE-PLATFORM-1).
- [ ] Loopback agent binds `127.0.0.1` only (NFR-4); honors idempotency (D5) + truthful failure mapping (D6); fabricates no success.
- [ ] `failure_domain=device` never populated (one-way path; DEC-001).

### WS6: Controlled print action + admin test-print
**Purpose:** Orchestrate write → submit → terminal; fail closed.
**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Flow: validate target+payload → resolve `instrument_ref` → `rpc_request_print_attempt` (server-derived key, DEC-005) → server-side cups adapter → loopback agent (DEC-002) → `rpc_transition_print_attempt`; fails closed before any success claim.
- [ ] **`printer_target_id` config source (DA §2):** resolved from server-side configuration ONLY (env var `LOYALTY_PRINT_AGENT_URL` + opaque `printer_target_id`; no DB table this pilot per `printer_target: configuration_only`). "Target available" is defined as the loopback agent health probe (`GET 127.0.0.1` agent `/health` → 200) succeeding before submission; an unreachable agent → `failed` / `failure_domain=transport_submission` (code `agent_unreachable`, DEC-006 — NOT a silent success, NOT a null domain). The raw agent URL/device address never reaches the browser.
- [ ] **`instrument_ref` resolution (P0-2/DEC-007):** the AUTHORITATIVE same-casino resolution lives in `rpc_request_print_attempt` (rejects non-resolving / cross-casino with `P0003` before any write). The route MAY perform an early read-only lookup purely for fast user-facing error mapping, but it is NOT the integrity boundary and is never relied upon as one.
- [ ] Render/validation failures caught BEFORE the adapter; persisted `failure_domain=render_validation`; `result_status=failed` (FR-6). Transport faults at/after submission map to `failed`/`transport_submission` (or `unknown` if post-submit ambiguous — DEC-006).
- [ ] Runtime guard: production action rejects any non-canonical (html-preview) renderer (FR-7) — runtime, not just lint.
- [ ] **Collision semantics (DA P1-B / DEC-005):** the `UNIQUE(casino_id, idempotency_key)` INSERT is the single-flight primitive — terminal prior row → return prior (no adapter call); in-flight (`requested`) prior row → reject (no adapter call). **The adapter is invoked EXACTLY ONCE, by the INSERT-winner only.** Explicit reprint nonce = new attempt sharing instrument identity (FR-5/GATE-DUP-1).
- [ ] NEVER writes `promo_coupon` / loyalty ledger on any outcome (GATE-DOM-1).
- [ ] No device address to the browser; opaque `printer_target_id` only; non-redeemable admin test-print path exercises the adapter DIRECTLY (no instrument, no `rpc_request_print_attempt`, no audit row).

### WS7: Operator UX — retire window.print() on named surface
**Purpose:** Duplicate-safe operator workflow; supersede `window.print()` on the named surface only.
**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] `issue-reward-button.tsx` performs **0** `window.print()` calls for BOTH families (entitlement + comp, DEC-003); 100% via controlled action (GATE-UX-1).
- [ ] `auto_attempt` on-issuance auto-print REMOVED from the controlled path (DEC-004); print fires only on explicit operator click.
- [ ] Bounded outcome badge (submitted/failed/unknown); copy never implies physical completion (submitted ≠ printed).
- [ ] **No Retry affordance on terminal outcomes (DEC-008):** `failed` and `unknown` are both terminal+immutable; the UI exposes NO "Retry" that re-drives the first_print key (it would only return the prior terminal row, or demand mutating a frozen row — forbidden by the WS1 trigger). Any second physical submission is the distinct, labelled, nonce-bearing **Reprint** only (DEC-005). No silent auto-retry; no automatic browser fallback.
- [ ] **`unknown` duplicate-risk (DEC-008):** because the job MAY have physically printed, Reprint on `unknown` requires an explicit duplicate-risk acknowledgement, and the badge states the outcome is unconfirmed. An optional non-printing **Refresh Status** action is permitted.
- [ ] `lib/print/` (incl. `auto_attempt`) untouched for non-loyalty docs.

### WS8: Test + boundary-lint suite
**Purpose:** Prove the platform exemplar contracts under real execution.
**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] One contract-parity suite both `cups` + `fake` pass returning canonical vocabulary; runs without a physical printer (GATE-PLATFORM-1/2).
- [ ] `ReceiptDocument` semantic snapshot (id, value, expiry, terms, token, template id/version, feed/cut).
- [ ] GATE-DOM-1 invariant: zero `promo_coupon` / ledger writes across all outcomes.
- [ ] GATE-DUP-1: idempotent replay = one copy; reprint = distinct lineage; **two concurrent first_print requests → adapter spy invoked exactly once** (DA P1-B).
- [ ] **Controlled-write boundary (P0-1/DEC-007):** a direct authenticated `.from('print_attempt').insert()` AND `.update()` are RLS-DENIED; the RPCs are the only write path.
- [ ] **instrument_ref resolution (P0-2/DEC-007):** a non-resolving / cross-casino `instrument_ref` is rejected by `rpc_request_print_attempt` (`P0003`) before any `print_attempt` write.
- [ ] **Comp-family parity (DA §7):** the `points_comp` (`ledger_entry`) family routes through the controlled path and persists a `print_attempt` with `instrument_kind=ledger_entry`.
- [ ] **Transport-failure domain (DEC-006):** an unreachable agent / spooler-reject transitions to `failed` / `failure_domain=transport_submission` (never a null domain on a transport fault).
- [ ] **Stale reconciliation (P0-4):** `rpc_mark_stale_print_attempts_unknown` sweeps a stale `requested` row to `unknown` and returns the count.
- [ ] **Integration tests actually execute (DA P1-A):** the `*.int.test.ts` files run under `jest.integration.config.js` with `RUN_INTEGRATION_TESTS=true` against a live stack — the `test-pass` gate fails closed if they are skipped / 0-total, and asserts every required int file was discovered (P1-2).
- [ ] Boundary lint (TEMPLATE-2/NFR-5): no Epson/ESC-POS/CUPS/spooler/`window.print` outside renderer/adapter.
- [ ] §7a deferral assertion: `failure_domain=device` never populated; PrinterFault vocabulary absent.

### WS9: E2E write-path (happy path)
**Purpose:** End-to-end proof of the controlled path.
**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Issue entitlement → controlled Print → `submitted` → `print_attempt` persisted with full correlation.

## Definition of Done (Gate E1 — mirrors PRD §8)

Functionality, Data & Integrity, Security & Access, Testing, Operational Readiness, Exemplar Proof,
Governance, Documentation — as enumerated in PRD-092 §8. The build is **Done** when all WS acceptance
criteria pass, the automatable gates are green, and the **manual GATE-HW-2** real-device exemplar is
recorded on the Linux/CUPS rig. Windows certification remains explicitly deferred (ADR-062 D8 anti-drift).
